87207(A)(1)

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

December 29, 1976

Mr. Carl Boronkay
Asst. General Counsel
Metropolitan Water District
of Southern California
1111 Sunset Boulevard
Post Office Box 54153
Los Angeles, California  90054

Re:  Proposed Change in Metropolitan's Conflict of Interest
and Disclosure of Personal Finances Code

Dear Mr. Boronkay:

     I am responding to your letter of December 9, 1976,
addressed to the Commissioners of the Fair Political Practices
Commission. In that correspondence you requested that the
requirement of gift disclosure in Section C2(a) of the Code
as approved be stricken.

     You indicate that this request was prompted by the board
of directors' determination that the "$25 threshold for report-
ing gifts is unreasonably burdensome and inappropriate for a
local agency such as Metropolitan".

     Government Code §87302(b) provides:

          The manner of reporting reportable items
          shall be substantially equivalent to the
          requirements of Article 2 of this chapter...

     The requirements of the Metropolitan Code, in Section
C2(a), parallel the requirements in Government Code §87207
(a)(1), thus fulfilling the "substantially equivalent"
standard of §87302(b). Furthermore, the Commission has
determined in connection with the review of the Conflict
of Interest Code of the Department of Justice that gift
disclosure at the statutory level is required for all
agencies adopting a Conflict of Interest Code.

     You ask, in addition, that approved Section C2(b) of
the Metropolitan Code be stricken.  That Section requires:

Carl Boronkay
December 29, 1976                                    Page Two


                    A statement whether the aggregate value of
              the income from each source was greater than
              $1,000 and whether it was greater than
              $10,000.

         The "substantially equivalent" standard of §87302(b)
    also mandates the disclosure of income in the manner pre-
    scribed in the Metropolitan Code, above.  Thus, both Sections
    C2(a) and C2(b) are proper and required provisions of the
    "manner of reporting reportable interests" of designated
    employees within the Conflict of Interest Code for the
    Metropolitan Water District.

         Your letter and this response will be reviewed by
    the Commission in its January 4-6 meeting.  If you have
    further questions, please feel free to call me at 916/
    322-6444 in Sacramento.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

    DL:glb

[The image appears to be almost entirely blank/white with only some very faint marks or artifacts visible at the top. There is no clearly legible text content to transcribe from this document image.]

The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California

December 9, 1976

CERTIFIED MAIL
RETURN RECEIPT REQUESTED

Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 8007
Sacramento, California  95804

Proposed Change in Metropolitan's
Conflict of Interest and Disclosure
of Personal Finances Code

Dear Commissioners:

     We have received the revisions to Metropolitan's pro-
posed Conflict of Interest and Disclosure of Personal Finances
Code. We understand the Code will be effective on January 1,
1977. Prior to implementation of the Code, however, we would
like to request a change in Section C on manner of reporting.
More specifically, we would request that in lines 3 and 4 of
Section C2(a) the words "or $25.00 or more in value if the in-
come was a gift" be stricken and that all of Section C2(d) be
stricken as well.

     This request was prompted by the Board of Directors'
determination that the $25.00 threshold for reporting gifts is
unreasonably burdensome and inappropriate for a local agency
such as Metropolitan. If this change is approved, it would make
gifts reportable at the same level as other income. We believe
this would still meet the requirements of Section 87300 et seq.
since the assurance would remain that potential conflicts would
be prevented by the disclosure of gifts.

     You are respectfully requested to advise us of your
decision in this matter on or before January 1, 1977, the effec-
tive date of the Code. Please consider this request without
prejudice to our earlier objections to the Commission's revi-
sions in the Code as proposed by Metropolitan.

     Thank you for your consideration of this matter.

Very truly yours,

Robert P. Will
General Counsel

Carl Boronkay
Assistant General Counsel

1111 Sunset Boulevard, Los Angeles, Calif. / Mailing address: Box 54153, Los Angeles, Calif. 90054 / Telephone: (213) 626-4232

I cannot reliably transcribe any clear text content from this document image.

FPPC
Received 9 or AH '76

The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California

Office of General Counsel                                December 9, 1976

CERTIFIED MAIL
RETURN RECEIPT REQUESTED

Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 8007
Sacramento, California  95804

                                    Proposed Change in Metropolitan's
                                    Conflict of Interest and Disclosure
                                    of Personal Finances Code

Dear Commissioners:

     We have received the revisions to Metropolitan's pro-
posed Conflict of Interest and Disclosure of Personal Finances
Code. We understand the Code will be effective on January 1,
1977. Prior to implementation of the Code, however, we would
like to request a change in Section C on manner of reporting.
More specifically, we would request that in lines 3 and 4 of
Section C2(a) the words "or $25.00 or more in value if the in-
come was a gift" be stricken and that all of Section C2(d) be
stricken as well.

     This request was prompted by the Board of Directors'
determination that the $25.00 threshold for reporting gifts is
unreasonably burdensome and inappropriate for a local agency
such as Metropolitan. If this change is approved, it would make
gifts reportable at the same level as other income. We believe
this would still meet the requirements of Section 87300 et seq.
since the assurance would remain that potential conflicts would
be prevented by the disclosure of gifts.

     You are respectfully requested to advise us of your
decision in this matter on or before January 1, 1977, the effec-
tive date of the Code. Please consider this request without
prejudice to our earlier objections to the Commission's revi-
sions in the Code as proposed by Metropolitan.

     Thank you for your consideration of this matter.

                                    Very truly yours,

                                    Robert P. Will
                                    General Counsel

                                    [signature]
                                    Carl Boronkay
                                    Assistant General Counsel

1111 Sunset Boulevard, Los Angeles, Calif. / Mailing address: Box 54153, Los Angeles, Calif. 90054 / Telephone: (213) 626-4282

In that correspondence you requested that the requirement of gift disclosure in Section C2(a) of the Code as approved be stricken. You indicate that this request was prompted by the board of directors' determination that the "$25 threshold for reporting gifts is unreasonably burdensome and inappropriate for a local agency such as Metropolitan". Government Code §87302(b) provides: The manner of reporting reportable items shall be substantially equivalent to the requirements of Article 2 of this chapter... The requirements of the Metropolitan Code, in Section C2(a), parallel the requirements in Government Code §87207 (a)(1), thus fulfilling the "substantially equivalent" standard of §87302(b). Furthermore, the Commission has determined in connection with the review of the Conflict of Interest Code of the Department of Justice that gift disclosure at the statutory level is required for all agencies adopting a Conflict of Interest Code. The "substantially equivalent" standard of §87302(b) also mandates the disclosure of income in the manner prescribed in the Metropolitan Code, above.
